strands.label: Local Command Execution

claims.grounding: user turn 2 shows explicit command invocation

claims.text: Directory /home/tony/projects was successfully added as working directory
claims.grounding: local-command-stdout confirms addition and save to local settings

claims.text: Permissions management is available for this working directory

strands.evolution: Initialized in cycle 1 from command execution logs

declared_losses.lost_information: Full semantic intent behind directory addition
declared_losses.reason: Command execution logs show WHAT occurred, not WHY. User motivation (project setup, organization, temporary workspace, etc.) is not present in the content.
declared_losses.recovery_path: Ask user explicitly if context becomes relevant

declared_losses.lost_information: Historical state of /home/tony/projects prior to command
declared_losses.reason: Stdout only confirms successful addition. No prior directory state, existing contents, or previous working directory status is recorded.
declared_losses.recovery_path: Irrelevant unless user queries directory history

open_questions: What is the user's intent for adding ~/projects as a working directory?
Does the user expect this directory to persist across sessions or is it temporary?
What role should permissions management play in subsequent operations?
instructions_for_next:
[
  "If user references 'projects' or 'working directory' in subsequent turns, maintain binding to /home/tony/projects",
  "If user issues commands involving file operations, assume /home/tony/projects is now a valid context root",
  "If permissions are mentioned, recall that /permissions management is available",
  "If user asks clarifying questions about the directory addition, you may lack intent context — note this honestly",
  "Monitor for additional /add-dir, /remove-dir, or /set-working-dir commands that would update the working directory state"
]
